# 🧪 PharmXChange Backend - Comprehensive Test Plan

## 📋 Overview
**Project**: PharmXChange Backend (Node.js/Express + PostgreSQL + Knex + Razorpay)  
**API Base**: `/api`  
**Auth**: JWT (access + refresh tokens) + Cookie-based + Role-based + Permission-based  
**Roles**: `buyer (role_id=1)`, `manufacturer (role_id=2)`, `admin (role_id=3)`, `superadmin (role_id=4)`

---

## 1️⃣ AUTHENTICATION FLOW (apis/auth/)

### 1.1 Register
**Endpoint**: `POST /api/auth/register`  
**Validator**: `authValidator.registerValidator`  
**Controller**: `authController.register` → `authService.register`

| ID | Test Case | Input | Expected Result | Notes |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------------|-------|
| AUTH-REG-001 | Register buyer with valid email | `{type:"email", value:"buyer@test.com", role:"buyer"}` | `201 {success:true, message:"OTP sent"}` | OTP stored in `user_otps` |
| AUTH-REG-002 | Register manufacturer with valid email | `{type:"email", value:"mfr@test.com", role:"manufacturer"}` | `201 {success:true, message:"OTP sent"}` | |
| AUTH-REG-003 | Register with invalid type | `{type:"phone", value:"abc@test.com", role:"buyer"}` | `422 {success:false, errors:[{field:"type", message:"Type must be email or mobile or whatsapp"}]}` | Validation |
| AUTH-REG-004 | Register with invalid email format | `{type:"email", value:"invalid", role:"buyer"}` | `422 {success:false, errors:[]}` | emailOrMobile custom validator |
| AUTH-REG-005 | Register with invalid role | `{type:"email", value:"buyer@test.com", role:"superhero"}` | `422 {success:false}` | Allowed: admin/buyer/manufacturer |
| AUTH-REG-006 | Register duplicate verified user | Same email + same role as existing verified user | `400 {success:false, message:"Account as buyer already exists..."}` | Check `authService.register` |
| AUTH-REG-007 | Register same email new OTP for unverified user | Email exists but `is_verified=false` | OTP refreshed, old OTPs invalidated | `authModel.invalidateOtps` |
| AUTH-REG-008 | Register with mobile (whatsapp type) | `{type:"whatsapp", value:"+919999999999", role:"buyer"}` | `201 {success:true}` | normalized to "mobile" |
| AUTH-REG-009 | Register buyer using mobile type | `{type:"mobile", value:"+919999999998", role:"buyer"}` | `201 {success:true}` | |
| AUTH-REG-010 | Register without value field | `{type:"email", role:"buyer"}` | `422` | Missing required field |

### 1.2 Resend OTP
**Endpoint**: `POST /api/auth/resend-otp`  
**Validator**: `authValidator.resendOtpValidator`

| ID | Test Case | Input | Expected Result | Notes |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------------|-------|
| AUTH-RESEND-001 | Resend OTP for existing user | `{identifier:"buyer@test.com", role_id:1}` | `200 {success:true, message:"OTP resent!"}` | Old OTPs invalidated → new OTP stored |
| AUTH-RESEND-002 | Resend OTP for non-existent user | `{identifier:"nobody@test.com"}` | `400 "User not found"` | |
| AUTH-RESEND-003 | Resend OTP for already verified user | Verified buyer email | `400 "User already verified"` | When purpose=REGISTER |
| AUTH-RESEND-004 | Resend OTP missing identifier | `{}` | `422` | Express-validator |

### 1.3 Verify OTP
**Endpoint**: `POST /api/auth/verify-otp`  
**Rate Limited**: `otpLimiter`  

| ID | Test Case | Input | Expected Result | Notes |

| E2E-004 | Manufacturer plan with seats | Login(role=2) → Create Order(MANUFACTURER_PLAN, seat_count=3) → Verify → Check subscription | Seats recorded |
| E2E-005 | Coin purchase flow | Login → Create Order(CUSTOM_COINS) → Verify → Check wallet | Balance incremented |
| E2E-006 | Clearance boost purchase | Login → Create Order(CLEARANCE_BOOST, tokens=5) → Verify → Check boost tokens | Token balance updated |
| E2E-007 | Full auth lifecycle: Register → Verify → Login → Refresh → Logout | Full chain | All tokens work, refresh rotates, logout revokes |
| E2E-008 | Forgot → Reset password flow | Login → Forgot password → Reset → Login with new password | Password reset successful |
| E2E-009 | Google login → subsequent refresh | Google login → Get refresh token → Refresh → New access token | Token rotation works |
| E2E-010 | Superadmin login with 2FA | Login → Receive OTP → Verify OTP → Full access | 2FA enforced |
| E2E-011 | Admin permission-based access | Admin with permissions → Access permitted route | Next() |
| E2E-012 | Admin without permission | Admin without specific permission | 403 |
| E2E-013 | Manufacturer seat user access (orgGuard) | Seat user accessing manufacturer route | OK |
| E2E-014 | Non-manufacturer accessing org-guarded route | Buyer accessing manufacturer route | 403 |
| E2E-015 | Payment → Refund flow (superadmin) | Login(superadmin) → Initiate refund → Check refund status → Verify wallet adjusted | |
| E2E-016 | Coin manual refund (superadmin) | Search user → Get wallet → Credit coins → Verify audit log → User notified | |
| E2E-017 | Subscription expiry → new plan queued | Buy plan with active subscription → New subscription is QUEUED | |
| E2E-018 | Non-domestic user pays in USD | User with foreign country → Create order → Currency = USD | |
| E2E-019 | Multiple failed login attempts → lockout | > N rapid failed logins | 429 |
| E2E-020 | Encrypted ID round-trip | Request with encrypted ID → Server decrypts → Response encrypts back | |

## 1. User Registration & Onboarding Flow

### Role IDs
| Role | ID |
|---|---|
| Buyer | 1 |
| Manufacturer | 2 |
| Admin | 3 |
| SuperAdmin | 4 |

### Registration Steps

1. **Frontend form**: Email, Mobile, Password, Full Name, Role ID, Business Category
2. **POST /api/auth/register**: Validates via `registerValidator` → Creates `users` record (is_verified=false) → Inserts OTP in `user_otps` → Sends OTP via email
3. **POST /api/auth/verify-otp**: Validates OTP via `otpLimiter` → Sets is_verified=true → Generates JWT tokens (access 15min, refresh 24h) → Sets httpOnly cookies → Emits `user:update` to admin socket room
4. **POST /api/auth/resend-otp**: Generate new OTP, replace old one

### Alternative: Google Login
- **POST /api/auth/google-login** — for Web Buyer
- **POST /api/auth/google-login-manufacturer** — for Web Manufacturer
- **POST /api/auth/google-login-mobile** — for Mobile
- Accepts `{ idToken, role }` → Creates/locates user by google_id → Returns tokens

### Token Security
- Access Token: 15 min expiry, httpOnly cookie + response body
- Refresh Token: 24h expiry, httpOnly cookie + hashed in `refresh_tokens` table
- Rotation: Old refresh token revoked upon each refresh
- Logout: Revokes refresh token + clears FCM token on user

### Key Auth Endpoints
| Method | Route | Rate Limited | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /api/auth/register | - | Register new user |
| POST | /api/auth/verify-otp | otpLimiter | Verify OTP & activate |
| POST | /api/auth/resend-otp | - | Resend OTP |
| POST | /api/auth/login | loginLimiter | Email + password login |
| POST | /api/auth/logout | - | Logout & revoke tokens |
| POST | /api/auth/change-password | - | Change password (auth) |
| POST | /api/auth/forgot-password | forgotPasswordLimiter | Send reset link |
| PUT | /api/auth/reset-password | forgotPasswordLimiter | Reset with token |
| POST | /api/auth/google-login* | - | Google OAuth (3 variants) |
| POST | /api/auth/refresh-token | - | Rotate tokens |
| GET | /api/auth/deeplink | - | Deep link redirect to app |

## 2. Authentication Flow

### Login Process
1. Validate input → Rate limit check via `loginLimiter`
2. Find user by email from `users` table
3. bcrypt.compare(password)
4. Check is_verified — if not, return `{ verification_required: true, user_id }`
5. Check is_active
6. Generate access_token + refresh_token
7. Store refresh_token hash in `refresh_tokens` table
8. Set httpOnly cookies (secure in prod, sameSite: "none" in prod)
9. Log event via `logContext(req, "LOGIN")`
10. Return `{ success, user, accessToken, refreshToken }`

### Token Refresh Process
1. Read refresh_token from cookie OR request body
2. Hash the token, find in refresh_tokens (check revoked & expiry)
3. Verify old token JWT signature
4. Revoke the old refresh token (rotation)
5. Issue new access token + refresh token pair
6. Set new cookies
7. Return new tokens + user

### Logout Process
1. Get refresh_token from cookie
2. Hash it, mark as revoked: true in DB
3. Clear access_token and refresh_token cookies
4. Clear fcm_token on user record
5. Log the logout event via logContext

### Auth Middleware Chain
```
Request → rateLimiter → verifyTokenAndAuthorizeRoles([allowed_ids]) → checkPermission("key") → Controller
```

### Auth Middleware Details
- **verifyTokenAndAuthorizeRoles([ids])**: Extracts JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header OR `access_token` cookie. Attaches `req.user = { id, email, role_id, role_name, manufacturer_id, manufacturer_role }`. Compares role_id against allowed array. Pass `[]` for any authenticated user.
- **authenticateOptionalToken**: Extracts token if present, doesn't fail if missing.
- **checkPermission("permission_key")**: Checks `user_permissions` table for granular feature access.

## 3. Password Reset Flow

### Forgot Password
```
POST /api/auth/forgot-password { email }
  → Validate email exists
  → Generate secure reset token
  → Send email with deep link URL
  → Deep link returns HTML redirect page:
     pharmxchangeapp://reset-password?token=xxx
  → Fallback: "Click here if app doesn't open"
```

### Reset Password
```
PUT /api/auth/reset-password { token, new_password }
  → Validate token (check expiry + match stored hash)
  → Hash new password
  → Update users.password
  → Revoke ALL refresh_tokens for that user (force re-login)
```

## 6. Payment & Subscription Flow

### Architecture
```
Frontend ↔ Express API ↔ Razorpay API (INR + USD instances)
               ↕
        paymentService.js (transactional logic)
               ↕
        PostgreSQL via Knex
               ↕
        Post-Payment: Notification, Socket, WhatsApp, Email PDF
```

### Purchase Types
| Type | What It Buys | Who Can Buy | Table Affected |
|---|---|---|---|
| BUYER_PLAN | Subscription plan + PharmX Coins | Buyer (role=1) | subscriptions, wallets, coin_transactions |
| MANUFACTURER_PLAN | Subscription plan + seats | Manufacturer (role=2) | subscriptions |
| MANUFACTURER_SEAT | Extra seat(s) for team | Manufacturer (role=2) | subscriptions.extra_seats_purchased |
| CUSTOM_COINS | PharmX Coin top-up | Any verified | wallets, coin_transactions |
| CLEARANCE_BOOST | Clearance boost tokens | Any verified | clearance_boost_tokens |
| BOOST_TOKEN | Visibility boost tokens | Any user | boost_tokens |

### Payment Flow - Phase 1: Create Order
**POST /api/payments/create-order** `{ purchase_type, plan_id?, coins?, seat_count?, tokens? }`

1. Validate user verification (skip for plan purchases)
2. Determine region (FOREIGN/NON_FOREIGN from country field)
3. Calculate amount based on purchase type and region rates
4. Enforce max amount (₹5L for INR coin purchases)
5. Create Razorpay order via razorpayINR or razorpayUSD instance
6. Store payment record in `payments` table with status="CREATED"
7. Return order_id, amount, currency, key_id to frontend

### Payment Flow - Phase 2: Verify Payment
**POST /api/payments/verify** `{ razorpay_order_id, razorpay_payment_id, razorpay_signature }`

1. HMAC-SHA256 signature verification (different secret for INR/USD)
2. Database transaction via `paymentService.processPaymentSuccess()`:
   - Update payment status to "PAID"
   - BUYER_PLAN: Check active sub → queue (if exists) or activate. Credit coins to wallet (if ACTIVE). Log coin_transaction.
   - MANUFACTURER_PLAN: Check active sub → queue or activate. Include (previous + new) extra_seats_purchased.
   - MANUFACTURER_SEAT: Increment extra_seats_purchased on active subscription.
   - CUSTOM_COINS: Increment wallet balance + log coin_transaction CREDIT.
   - CLEARANCE_BOOST: tokenService.addTokens() to clearance_boost tokens.
   - BOOST_TOKEN: tokenService.addTokens() to boost tokens.

### Payment Flow - Phase 3: Post-Payment
1. sendNotification(): In-app notification with type="PAYMENT_SUCCESS"
2. Socket.IO: payment:success + dashboard:tickers_updated to user room
3. WhatsApp: Send confirmation template for plan/seat purchases
4. Email: Generate PDF invoice, attach and send

### Webhook Alternative
**POST /api/payments/webhook** (express.raw for signature verification)
- Events: payment.captured, refund.processed, refund.failed, refund.created

### Subscription States
| State | Description |
|---|---|
| ACTIVE | Currently benefiting from plan |
| QUEUED | Scheduled to start after current period ends |
| EXPIRED | Past end_date, no longer active |
| CANCELLED | Manually cancelled by user/admin |

### Currency Handling
- INR for Indian users (NON_FOREIGN region)
- USD for International users (FOREIGN region)
- Plan currency must match user's region
- Exchange rate snapshot stored in payments for audit

## 7. RFQ (Request for Quotation) Flow

### What is RFQ?
Buyers create RFQs for pharmaceutical products. Manufacturers view matched RFQs, submit quotes, negotiate, and win deals. Core B2B matching feature.

### RFQ Lifecycle States
```
DRAFT → OPEN → QUOTED → NEGOTIATION → AWARDED
                ↘                    ↘
                CLOSED               CLOSED
```

### Key RFQ Endpoints
| Method | Route | Role | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /api/rfqs | Buyer | Create new RFQ |
| GET | /api/rfqs | Buyer | List my RFQs |
| GET | /api/rfqs/:id | Buyer/Admin | Get RFQ details |
| PUT | /api/rfqs/:id | Buyer | Update RFQ |
| DELETE | /api/rfqs/:id | Buyer | Delete RFQ |
| POST | /api/rfqs/:id/negotiate | Buyer/Mfg | Send negotiation msg |
| POST | /api/rfqs/:id/reveal | Manufacturer | Pay coins to reveal buyer |
| POST | /api/manufacturer/rfqs/:rfqId/quote | Manufacturer | Submit a quote |
| GET | /api/manufacturer/rfqs | Manufacturer | List matched RFQs |
| GET | /api/rfqs/ratings | Both | Submit/view ratings |

### Creating an RFQ (Step by Step)
1. Validate all master data IDs
2. Upload documents via multer
3. INSERT rfqs with status="OPEN"
4. INSERT rfq_snapshots (initial state JSONB)
5. INSERT rfq_history (action="CREATED")
6. Match manufacturers (categories, therapeutics, molecules, countries)
7. INSERT rfq_matches
8. Notify matched manufacturers
9. Socket: rfq:update to admin

### Quoting (Manufacturer Side)
**POST /api/manufacturer/rfqs/:rfqId/quote** `{ unit_price, currency, moq, lead_time, incoterm, valid_until, documents }`
1. INSERT rfq_quotes
2. Update status to "QUOTED"
3. Notify buyer

### Reveal (Manufacturer Pays Coins)
**POST /api/rfqs/:rfqId/reveal**
1. Check coin balance
2. Deduct coins
3. INSERT rfq_reveal_requests
4. Return buyer details

### RFQ Data Model (Key Tables)
- **rfqs**: id, buyer_id, product_name, dosage_form_id, package_type_id, quantity, unit, status, value, buying_intent, delivery_address(JSON), cosmetic_fields(JSON), validity_days
- **rfq_matches**: id, rfq_id, manufacturer_id, match_type(SYSTEM/MANUAL)
- **rfq_quotes**: id, rfq_id, manufacturer_id, unit_price, currency, moq, lead_time, incoterm, valid_until, quote_type
- **rfq_negotiations**: id, rfq_id, quote_id, sender_type, message, counter_price
- **rfq_reveal_requests**: id, rfq_id, manufacturer_id, coin_cost, status
- **rfq_history**: id, rfq_id, action, old_status, new_status, changed_by
- **rfq_snapshots**: id, rfq_id, data(JSONB)
- **rfq_views**, rfq_chat_read_states, rfq_negotiation_read_states

### RFQ Master Tables
master_rfq_dosage_forms, master_rfq_dosage_styles, master_rfq_package_types, master_rfq_package_styles, master_rfq_package_forms, master_rfq_add_ons, master_export_country_accreditations, master_export_documents_required, master_export_shipping_terms, master_business_categories, master_business_therapeutics, master_business_molecules

## 8. Clearance Listings Flow

### What are Clearance Listings?
Sellers list distressed/overstock/clearance inventory at discounted prices. Marketplace for surplus pharma stock.

### Listing States
```
DRAFT → ACTIVE → SOLD
              → EXPIRED (cron)
              → CANCELLED
```

### Key Endpoints
| Method | Route | Role | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /api/clearance/listings | Any verified | Create listing |
| GET | /api/clearance/listings/my | Seller | My listings |
| GET | /api/clearance/listings/:id | Any | Listing detail |
| PUT | /api/clearance/listings/:id | Seller | Update listing |
| DELETE | /api/clearance/listings/:id | Seller | Cancel listing |
| GET | /api/clearance/market | Public | Browse market (search, filter, sort) |

### Create Listing Flow
1. Validate user is verified
2. Upload images via multer (public/clearance_boosts/)
3. INSERT clearance_listings (status="ACTIVE")
4. Socket: listing:update to admin

### Browse Market
**GET /api/clearance/market** with search, filter, pagination
- Query with JOINs to users, images
- Include boost status for ranking
- Paginate via knex-paginate

**POST /api/clearance/chat/:chatId/messages** `{ content, message_type, file? }`

103: 1. Read refresh_token from cookie OR request body

111: ### Logout Process
112: 1. Get refresh_token from cookie
113: 2. Hash it, mark as revoked: true in DB
114: 3. Clear access_token and refresh_token cookies

118: ### Auth Middleware Chain
119: ```
120: Request → rateLimiter → verifyTokenAndAuthorizeRoles([allowed_ids]) → checkPermission("key") → Controller

123: ### Auth Middleware Details
124: - **verifyTokenAndAuthorizeRoles([ids])**: Extracts JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header OR `access_token` cookie. Attaches `req.user = { id, email, role_id, role_name, manufacturer_id, manufacturer_role }`. Compares role_id against allowed array. Pass `[]` for any authenticated user.
125: - **authenticateOptionalToken**: Extracts token if present, doesn't fail if missing.
126: - **checkPermission("permission_key")**: Checks `user_permissions` table for granular feature access.

131: 
132: ### Forgot Password
133: ```
134: POST /api/auth/forgot-password { email }
135:   → Validate email exists
136:   → Generate secure reset token
137:   → Send email with deep link URL
138:   → Deep link returns HTML redirect page:
139:      pharmxchangeapp://reset-password?token=xxx
140:   → Fallback: "Click here if app doesn't open"
141: ```
142: 
143: ### Reset Password
144: ```
145: PUT /api/auth/reset-password { token, new_password }
146:   → Validate token (check expiry + match stored hash)
147:   → Hash new password
148:   → Update users.password
149:   → Revoke ALL refresh_tokens for that user (force re-login)
150: ```

154: ## 4. Buyer Profile Setup & Verification Flow
155: 
156: ### Profile Endpoints
157: | Method | Route | Auth | Description |
158: |---|---|---|---|
159: | GET | /api/user/profile | All roles | Get profile + buyer_profiles |
160: | PUT | /api/user/profile | All roles | Update profile fields |
161: | POST | /api/user/profile-pic | All roles | Upload profile image |
162: | GET | /api/user/business-categories | Public | List all categories |
163: | PUT | /api/user/gift-modal-seen | All roles | Mark gift modal as seen |
164: | PUT | /api/user/disclaimer-signed | All roles | Mark disclaimer signed |
165: | POST | /api/user/fcm-token | All roles | Save FCM push token |
166: | GET | /api/user/digilocker/auth-url | All roles | Get DigiLocker OAuth URL |
167: | GET/POST | /api/user/digilocker/webhook | Public | DigiLocker callback |
168: | DELETE | /api/user/profile | All roles | Self-delete account |
169: | DELETE | /api/user/:id | Admin/SuperAdmin | Admin delete user |
170: 
171: ### Verification Process
172: 1. Register → OTP Verify → user created with role_id=1
173: 2. Complete profile → company_name, gst_number, business_category_id, therapeutic_categories → stored in `buyer_profiles`
174: 3. Upload certificates → business certs, category certs, GST documents → stored in buyer_profiles fields
175: 4. (Optional) DigiLocker integration → OAuth + webhook callback
176: 5. Admin reviews via /api/admin/verification
177: 6. On approval: buyer_profiles.verification_status = "VERIFIED" + users.is_verified = true

181: ## 5. Manufacturer Profile Setup & Verification Flow
182: 
183: ### Key Route Groups
184: | Route Prefix | Description |
185: |---|---|
186: | /api/manufacturer (profile) | Company profile CRUD |
187: | /api/manufacturer/compliance | Certificate upload & management |
188: | /api/manufacturer/seat-user | Seat user invitation & management |
189: | /api/manufacturer/plants | Manufacturing plants CRUD |
190: | /api/manufacturer/rfqs | RFQ response, quoting |
191: | /api/manufacturer/internal-chat | Internal team chat |
192: | /api/manufacturer/support | Support ticket system |
193: | /api/manufacturer/dashboard | Dashboard card counts |
194: 
195: ### Verification Steps
196: 1. Register → OTP Verify → creates user with role_id=2 + empty `manufacturers` record
197: 2. Complete company profile: company_name, registration_number, country, currency, legal_entity_type, business_type
198: 3. Upload certificates: GMP, ISO, FSSAI, WHO-GMP → stored in `manufacturer_certificates` with file_url + expiry_date
199: 4. Register plants: name, address, certifications
200: 5. Admin reviews all documents via /api/admin/verification
